02/16/1990  1b:48  SEC UP STATE EXEC OFFICE  8 454 4573  06B04506  P.02

Office of the Secretary of State    Executive Office              (916) 445-6371
March Fong Eu                        1230 J Street
                                     Sacramento, California 95814


                                     February 16, 1990


VIA FAX

Ms. Margaret Ellison
Fair Political Practices Commission
428 J Street, Suite 800
Sacramento, California 95814

Re:  June 1990 Primary Election Ballot Pamphlet

Dear Margaret:

     This is to confirm our conversation of today regarding the
application of Commission Regulation 18901 (mass mailings
sent at public expense) to the California Ballot
Pamphlet. Your prompt response to my inquiry is very much
appreciated.

     Pursuant to our discussion, we have deleted all references
to "March Fong Eu" and "Secretary of State" in the
pamphlet except as they appear in the "Certificate of
Correctness" on the front cover, in the letterhead on page
two, the return address on the back cover, and the postal
permit (name of sender required by postal regulations).
(See enclosed.)

     Please advise whether these modifications are consistent
with our discussion.

     Again, thank you for your timely advice.

                                     Sincerely,


                                     ANTHONY L. MILLER
                                     Chief Deputy

Enclosures




FEB 16 '90 15:48           8 454 4573  06B0450E  PAGE.002

02/16/1990    10:57  SEC OF STATE EXEC OFFICE   8 454 4573  U66U45D6    P.02


                          Office of the Secretary of State      Executive Office                    (816) 445-6371
                          March Fong Eu                         1230 J Street
                                                                Sacramento, California 95814


                                                          February 16, 1990


Gregory Baugher, Executive
Director
Fair Political Practices Commission
428 J Street, Suite 800
Sacramento, California 95814

Re: Request for Informal Advice: June 1990 Primary
     Election Ballot Pamphlet

Dear Greg:

     On July 25, 1988, I was advised by your staff (File No.
A-88-257) that the Secretary of State's "certificate of
correctness" required pursuant to Government Code section
88005(d) could continue to include the signature of the
Secretary of State in the wake of Proposition 73.  The
November 1988 General Election Ballot Pamphlet was
formatted accordingly.  No other reference to the name of
the Secretary of State was included in the pamphlet.

     In preparing the June 1990 Primary Election Ballot
Pamphlet which was placed on public display today pursuant
to Government Code section 88006, we have followed the
November 1988 format.  The name of the Secretary of State
is included only in the "certificate of correctness".
(See enclosed pages.)  Please advise as soon as possible
if this format does not, for any reason, comply with the
Political Reform Act as amended by Proposition 73.

                                        Sincerely,

                                        [signature]

                                        ANTHONY L. MILLER
                                        Chief Deputy

Enclosures


[seal]

02/16/1930    10:59   SEC OF STATE EXEC OFFICE   8 454 4573  05604506   P.03

California

BALLOT PAMPHLET

EUREKA

Primary
Election

JUNE 5, 1990

CALIFORNIA

CERTIFICATE OF SECRETARY OF STATE

I, March Fong Eu, Secretary of State of the State of California, do hereby certify that the foregoing is
measured will be submitted to the electors of the State of California at the PRIMARY ELECTION to be
held throughout the State on        JUNE 5, 1990   and that this pamphlet has been correctly prepared in
accordance with law.

Witness my hand and the Great Seal of the State in
Sacramento, California this  •  day of  •

March Fong Eu

MARCH FONG EU
Secretary of State

place to be applied following period of public display and
any subsequent court ordered changes.

Secretary of State
SACRAMENTO 95814

Dear Fellow Californians:

This is your California Ballot Pamphlet for the June 5, 1990,
Primary Election. It contains the ballot title, a short summary, the
Legislative Analyst's analysis, the pro and con arguments and
rebuttals, and the complete text of each proposition. It also
contains the legislative vote cast for and against each measure
proposed by the Legislature.

Many rights and responsibilities go along with citizenship.
Voting is one of the most important, as it is the foundation on
which our democratic system is built. Read carefully all of the
measures and information about them contained in this pamphlet. Legislative propositions and citizen-sponsored initiatives
are designed specifically to give you, the electorate, the opportunity to influence the laws which regulate us all.

Take advantage of this opportunity and exercise your rights by
voting on June 5, 1990.

SECRETARY OF STATE
